text.title: Image Generation Basics Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules for production image-generation pipelines (DALL-E 3 + Flux + SDXL).

rule.statement: Prompts are assembled via a structured builder (subject + style + lighting + composition + technical). Free-text concatenation is rejected.
rule.rationale: Unstructured prompts drift; the builder forces consistency and is diffable.

rule.statement: Log response.revised_prompt for every DALL-E 3 call. Compare to input on-demand.
rule.rationale: DALL-E 3 silently rewrites prompts; without logging the audit trail is lost.

rule.statement: Download image bytes to durable storage within the call; URLs expire in ~1h.
rule.rationale: Pipelines that cache URLs and resume later get 404s.

rule.statement: Respect provider rate limits with exponential backoff; tier-1 DALL-E 3 = 5 imgs/min per org (not per key).
rule.rationale: Shared-org keys silently throttle concurrent agents.

rule.statement: Smoke-test the first 3 outputs of any new prompt set before launching a 100+ image batch.
rule.rationale: Content-policy refusals or revised-prompt drift surface here; catching at 3 saves 97 wasted calls.

rule.statement: Route by cost-tolerance: DALL-E 3 for tight-brand low-volume; Flux-schnell or SDXL via Replicate for cost-sensitive high-volume.
rule.rationale: DALL-E 3 at $0.04-$0.12/image is 100x Replicate; using it for high-volume is a finops anti-pattern.

rule.statement: Preconditions hold → wire generation pipeline per procedure.
Pixel-perfect brand required OR real-person likeness OR on-image text → skip.
